item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: scaramouche, scaramouche, can you do the fandango?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The incident report states ". The depletion of our realm's heat is eroding the fundamental bedrock upon which all probability rests, you must understand that!"

Draining Hell of its heat roughly results in creating a metaphorical sinkhole that destorys the laws of reality itself. "When pigs fly" and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, it's very good, but… I don't know. Something feels a little off. I guess it feels… unfinished. Open-ended.

Hell's freezing over. Okay. Why should the Foundation care? Like, they don't do shit unless they absolutely have to - they are pretty much the most cost-stringent doomsday-preventers imaginable, and while they will splurge when necessary, this… doesn't seem that necessary. What exactly does Hell freezing over entail? That much really isn't made clear.

No-vote from me, but I'll think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My +1 just raised this article to +66. I feel…proud?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do find -2 to be too silly, like the article is trying too hard to have a wacky casual character.

With that said, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no objections to humorous articles in the main list - I've upvoted 999, for example - but this goes clean past humorous and into silly. A joke might be OK if it leads into black comedy or the like, but here it's just a punchline.

With some tweaking it'd be an excellent -J, but I'm just not liking it here. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to SunnyClockwork for some awesome fanart of Raum!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are parts of this I really don't like. But I'm going to upvoted it because it did make me smile. -2's flipping of mannerisms was pretty funny.

Maybe some of the dialogue could be less silly. I was unimpressed with the Hot Topic line. But it's fine as is, for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3304-2: Oh shit, my good. Citizens United doesn't happen until next week.
[…]
Dietz: Yes, that's all well and good but… you did all this to make money right? If you can see the future, why bother with the thermal plant? You could have just bought a winning lottery ticket or bet on a horse race or something.

SCP-3304-2: … Oh God fucking bless it.

Upvoted primarily for this exchange, which grabbed me from the start and doesn't overstay its welcome.

Also that last line had me snickering into my glass of juice so good work sir +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a glorious feghoot and you should be both ashamed and proud.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Articles on this site are about the writing, and if that writing entertained or impressed you.

That's not 100% accurate, though. There are plenty of tales on this site that are entertaining and impressive writing, but if they were uploaded (unedited) in a mainlist SCP slot, they would be downvoted.

My point is: there are different expectations for different types of submissions to the site. "Entertaining or impressive" is (hopefully) necessary for success, but it is not sufficient.

In my example above of tales posted on the mainlist, one of the other necessary requirements - the standard mainlist format - is missing, and therefore downvotes accrue.

In the case of this article, I downvoted it because the Foundation's unserious attitude, and that of the demons, destroyed my suspension of disbelief. I'm fine with humour in mainlist articles (and have upvoted many examples), provided that suspension of belief remains largely intact. Suspension of disbelief in the shared setting is (for me) another necessary element of a successful mainlist skip.

That same suspension of disbelief is not necessary (in my mind) when it comes to -J articles, which serve a different purpose, and are assessed against different criteria. As such, I have in the past downvoted articles on the mainlist that I might upvote as -Js. I suspect that others apply a similar criteria, with similar results.

As such, I don't agree with you that we're looking to "limit creativity" by downvoting (some) humorous writing on the mainlist. We're simply assessing different subsets of articles on the site by different criteria. That's what the "arbitrary classifications" mean in the first place! Otherwise we could just have one big list of articles on the site, without differentiating mainlisters from tales from -Js from -EXs from GoI formats - and I don't think you're suggesting that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to upvote anyway, but "what kind of Hot Topic bullshit is this?" made it obligatory.

The idea of energy transfer between worlds, the commercial exploitation thereof, and contract law negotiations between the Foundation and a Prince of Hell all coming together to justify that last sentence - that kind of wonderful is what keeps me coming back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ngl I really don’t like when people say stuff like “I would have upvoted if it was a J” because that kinda misses the point. Articles on this site are about the writing, and if that writing entertained or impressed you. The arbitrary classification of an article itself shouldn’t matter for the most part. If you thought this wasn’t funny or wasn’t good, by all means downvote. I’m well aware my comedic style is divisive to say the least. But if you liked the article, if you laughed, evaluate it for what it is rather than if it has a -J next to its title. Trying to restrict all humorous writing to Js just limits creativity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I generally agree with this, but I feel that the excellent execution and interesting twist saves it from a downvote. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

No, no. Much too silly! https://youtu.be/UIVilRyWzO4

Seriously, might upvote for a -J, but the demon's personality was just too over the top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice catch, fixed. Re: logs I don’t think that would really add anything tbh. This is basically a short and silly piece, kinda like 2337 or the recent one with the incest loving cacti.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is defintely a humorous article, but there’s a long tradition of those by people like Daveyoufool and Zoltan. Humor isn’t restricted wholly to Js.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think in the second incident report you used SCP-3304-2 when you meant to use SCP-738.

Other than that, I like the idea, but the idea itself is a bit silly. The conclusion came a bit fast. Maybe flesh out the development after SCP-3304 gets into Foundation custody a bit with some experiment logs or incident logs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m torn here. On one hand, this does have a nice sense of humour to it, and that final little note is a nice little ‘oomph’ as it were—and honestly, I do like in a twisted how the SCP Foundation is on speaking terms and generally friendly relations with literally Hell itself.

On the other hand though, it feels a bit too silly, at least for a Mainline article. Like, this has all the kind if set up I feel would make for a good Tale or so, but some part of me—maybe some ancient part that still insists that the SCP is a “Seriosus business of Horror and Inexplicable Spooky stuff”—can’t come to grips with this.

I may change this opinion in the future, but for now, I’m begrudgingly staying Neutral on this. It’s good, but it feels just too Silly—a good kind of Silly, but one I’m torn on supporting. Goos job all the same, however.

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
All credit to Hippo for the original idea, which I then endeavored to make as stupid as possible :P

If you enjoyed, check out my other stuff here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/modern-erasmus-author-page

Raum is based on the demon Raum from the Ars Goetia.
